name: Bangladesh
experiences: Old Dhaka biryani and riverfront heritage
Cox's Bazar beach and Marine Drive
Sylhet tea gardens and Lawachara rainforest
avoid: April–June is hot and humid across Dhaka, Rajshahi and Cox's Bazar, with dusty afternoons inland.
June–September monsoon can flood low-lying roads, roughen sea conditions in Cox's Bazar and disrupt Sundarbans boat departures.

cities.name: Dhaka
cities.notes: Old Dhaka heritage, Mughal forts, river-port energy and the country's best classic biryani stops

cities.name: Cox's Bazar
cities.notes: Long sandy beach, Marine Drive viewpoints, seafood restaurants and easy side trips to Himchari and Inani

cities.name: Sundarbans
cities.notes: Khulna-Mongla gateway for mangrove creeks, wildlife watchtowers, Karamjal boardwalks and riverboat nights

cities.name: Sylhet
cities.notes: Tea estates, shrine visits, Ratargul wetlands and the Lawachara rainforest around Sreemangal

cities.name: Rajshahi (Paharpur)
cities.notes: Padma-side Rajshahi base for silk shops, Varendra heritage and the UNESCO Buddhist ruins at Paharpur

itinerary.sim: Grameenphone or Robi prepaid SIM is the easiest setup; expect roughly ₹225–₹525 for the SIM plus a 15–30 day data pack with enough maps and ride-hailing data.

Dhaka.name: Dhaka
Dhaka.note: Two days work well for Old Dhaka's headline monuments, a food-heavy riverfront circuit and one modern-city museum stop before you fly onward.

days.theme: Arrival, Old Dhaka Forts & Pink Palace

activities.name: Airport transfer by CNG auto-rickshaw or app cab into central Dhaka
Lalbagh Fort
activities.cost: ₹75 pp for SAARC visitors / ₹150 pp for other foreign visitors
activities.tip: The published ticket rates are BDT 100 for SAARC and BDT 200 for other foreign travelers, so carry small cash for the counter.

activities.name: Ahsan Manzil Museum
activities.cost: ₹225 pp for SAARC visitors / ₹375 pp for other foreign visitors
activities.tip: Current ticketing is BDT 300 for SAARC and BDT 500 for other foreign visitors; Fridays and public holidays are the busiest.

activities.name: Shankhari Bazar and Armenian Church lane walk
Old Dhaka biryani and lacchi crawl
Liberation War Museum
Jatiya Sangsad Bhaban exterior and Crescent Lake walk
Dhakeshwari National Temple
Sadarghat riverfront and historic BIWTC Rocket ghat walk
CNG auto-rickshaw hops between sights

Cox's Bazar.name: Cox's Bazar
Cox's Bazar.note: Two days cover the main beach strip plus one Marine Drive outing to Himchari and Inani without turning the stay into only transit.

days.theme: Biman Bangladesh Flight, Beach Check-in & Sunset

activities.name: Biman Bangladesh Airlines or US-Bangla domestic flight from Dhaka to Cox's Bazar
activities.tip: Biman is the most reliable full-service domestic option in peak season; morning departures preserve most of your beach day.

activities.name: Laboni Beach and Kolatoli sea-front walk
Burmese Market snack and souvenir stop
Sugandha Point sunset
Reserve CNG auto-rickshaw or jeep along Marine Drive
Himchari National Park viewpoint
Inani Beach coral-stone shoreline stop
Seafood lunch by the beach
Laboni or Kolatoli night market return

Sundarbans.name: Sundarbans
Sundarbans.note: A two-day Khulna-Mongla safari is the shortest worthwhile version because permit formalities and tidal boat timings consume most of day one.

activities.name: Shyamoli NR Travels or BRTC coach from Dhaka to Khulna
Shared transfer onward to Mongla jetty
Bangladesh Forest Department Sundarbans permit
activities.cost: ₹375 pp for Karamjal day entry or ₹1,725–₹2,588 pp/day for sanctuary permits with VAT
activities.tip: Recent published rates are BDT 500 for Karamjal foreign entry, BDT 2,000 outside sanctuary zones and BDT 3,000 inside sanctuary zones, before 15% VAT.

activities.name: Karamjal Wildlife Centre boardwalk
Boat safari through the Mongla-Karamjal creek network
activities.cost: ₹4,500–₹9,000 pp on a shared operator tour

days.theme: Harbaria Trail, Watchtowers & Return to Khulna

activities.name: Harbaria eco-trail or Kotka/Kochikhali wildlife watchtower cruise
activities.cost: Usually bundled into your shared boat package

activities.name: Forest guide and guard charges
activities.tip: Guides are typically charged at BDT 750 per person per day and forest guards at BDT 500 per guard per day, usually split across the boat.

activities.name: Birding and mudflat dolphin watch from the launch deck
Khulna riverside dinner after return

Sylhet.name: Sylhet
Sylhet.note: Two days let you split shrine-and-city time from a proper Sreemangal or Lawachara excursion rather than cramming both into one road-heavy day.

activities.name: Biman Bangladesh domestic flight into Sylhet via Dhaka if needed
Hazrat Shah Jalal Mazar Sharif complex
Ratargul Swamp Forest shared boat
Kin Bridge and Surma riverfront evening walk
Road or rail transfer to Sreemangal / Lawachara National Park
Lawachara National Park entry
activities.tip: The current published foreigner fee is BDT 1,150; locals pay far less, so cash is useful at the gate.

activities.name: Kalabon trail with local guide
Nilkantha Tea Cabin layered tea stop
Madhabpur Lake or tea-estate photo detour

Rajshahi (Paharpur).name: Rajshahi (Paharpur)
Rajshahi (Paharpur).note: Two days are ideal because Paharpur is a long archaeological side trip from Rajshahi and pairs best with one slower city day on the Padma riverfront.

activities.name: Intercity transfer by coach or flight via Dhaka into Rajshahi
Varendra Research Museum
Rajshahi Silk Factory outlet and New Market shopping
Padma river embankment sunset walk
Private car or bus excursion to Somapura Mahavihara, Paharpur
Somapura Mahavihara entry
activities.tip: Recent traveler reports place foreign and SAARC tickets around BDT 200–300, usually with the site museum included.

activities.name: Site museum and monastery mound circuit
Bagha Mosque stop on the return drive
Rajshahi dinner back in town

connections.method: Biman Bangladesh Airlines or US-Bangla nonstop domestic flight (about 1 hour)
Fly back to Dhaka, then continue by Shyamoli NR / BRTC coach to Khulna and a short transfer to Mongla
Return to Khulna or Jashore, then connect via Dhaka by Biman Bangladesh or an overnight AC coach
Biman Bangladesh domestic routing via Dhaka, or a slower rail-plus-road combination

extras: Classic BIWTC Rocket steamers on the Dhaka-Hularhat route are currently suspended, so plan on modern Sadarghat launches if you want a heritage-style river journey.
Cards work in major hotels, but carry ₹2,000–₹4,000 worth of taka in cash-equivalent for CNG rides, museum counters, market snacks and park gates.
Cox's Bazar and Sundarbans departures fill quickly in December-January weekends, so lock domestic flights and boat packages early.
